seoTitle: How to Connect Google Drive to Slack (2026)
metaDescription: Link Google Drive to Slack for automatic file previews and comment alerts, so shared links unfurl right in your chat feed.
intro: Sharing file links and managing access without leaving Slack becomes much easier once Google Drive is properly connected. This guide covers installing the official app, authorizing your Google account, and testing automatic link previews.

steps.heading: Open the App Directory
steps.body: From the Tools section of your Slack workspace, launch the App Directory panel.

steps.heading: Search for Google Drive
steps.body: Type Google Drive into the search field and select the official app profile.

steps.heading: Add the app to Slack
steps.body: Click 'Add to Slack' to start the cloud integration wizard.

steps.heading: Authorize your Google account
steps.body: Review the requested permissions, click Allow, then sign in to your Google account when redirected.

steps.heading: Test link unfurling
steps.body: Paste a Google Doc link into a channel to confirm Slack automatically generates a rich preview card.

faq.question: Will Slack be able to access all my Drive files?
faq.answer: Slack only accesses files you explicitly share as links inside the workspace, not your entire Drive by default.

faq.question: Can I grant document access directly from Slack?
faq.answer: Yes, the integration supports in-app permission prompts so you can grant view access to channel members without leaving Slack.

faq.question: Will I get notified about comments on a shared document?
faq.answer: Yes, once connected, you can receive alerts for comments and access requests directly in your Slack sidebar.

faq.question: Does this work with a personal Gmail account or only Workspace accounts?
faq.answer: Both personal Google accounts and Google Workspace accounts can typically be connected.
